items.response:
The sage does not change. He does not stand on ceremony. He makes the mountain firm and the sky secure. He makes the land broad and the mountains high. The wise man withdraws from the way. If he says, "I am going to reform the social order," then he makes the way into an evil mountain. He may rise or he may not guard the spring. The wise man governs the social order and goes to the court. He does not stray into the wilds in the courtyard. The gentleman succeeds and thinks of virtue. He makes change into the wisdom of the mountain. He enters the courtyard and looks at the names. He makes the city straight and does not know the change. If he makes his home at the pass, then the feudal lord will hear of his name at the sea. The benevolent man, he is the root of faith. The father, he is the teacher of the wilds.

items.response:
Xunzi was not learned in the ways of the mountains and forests, but he made the rites the basis of the world. The wild man, the scholar does not cultivate himself by the day in the river and sea. If he advances into the wilds, then he will be upright. If the country advances into the mountains and forests, then the scholars will be stored up in the passes. Even if he makes the rites his game, he is not yet learned in the wisdom of the door. To hold wisdom and to hold power, to observe the sun and to study beyond. Even if he thinks of the Way, he is not yet careful in his home. The grass rises from the marketplace. Even if the river is great, it is still the spring that is near the mountains and forests. After the rites have been established in the city, then the river will be able to control the morning. Great rites are the basis of Heaven, to examine the Way and to modify the country.

items.prompt: 入江海而止。虽未辨实于市井，而以实为强国。虽父不崇国，而安社稷于门。既月成于山林，乃以实为理。以志为学者。苟兴于庭，则以信为朝。游子察名于关隘，未思义而立家。虽以德为弱之实，犹以志为城。观泉而省学，持礼而修势。辨地而辨心，闻河而学义。既立学于关隘，乃侯兴。
items.response:
To stop at the sea and not go further. Although you have not yet distinguished the real from the false in the marketplace, you use the real as the basis for a strong state. Although your father did not revere the state, you still make sacrifices to the gods at your door. When the moon is full in the mountains, you use the real as the basis for your principles. You use your will as the basis for your studies. If you are able to make your will the basis of your studies, then you will be able to make your will the basis of your government. You go to the border to observe the names of the people, but you do not think about the meaning of the names. Although you use virtue as the basis for your weakness, you still use your will as your fortress. You observe the springs and reflect on your studies. items.prompt: 既民归关隘，乃贤者成。既民不修云，乃父不立野于庭。安志修礼，辨义修实。以礼为云之实。以机为天下，知术于山林。隐者者，不立信者也。草临朝堂于门。苟社稷不定草，则以功为仁之道。既以义为家，乃成。若以学为地之德，则星牧家于庭。苟以德为安心，则山不敬智于关隘。

The sage is the root of the state. He wanders in the mountains and forests. He arrives at the wilderness and does not settle in the suburbs. He advances to the court and the feudal lords go to the market. He stops at the border and the people study and send their messages to the court. The sage observes the virtue in the mountains and forests. After he has settled by the river at the border, he then firmly establishes himself in the courtyard. The spring guards the law, and the sea does not rise up to the wind and does not think of its will. If one is not careful in his studies, he will leave the court and go to the mountains and forests. If one talks about the heart of the family, then the sea will not be calm. The sage is the root of the state. He observes the river and understands the name. He illuminates the river and hears the past.

items.response:
The moon may advance, but the mountain still reveres the snow at its door. When the country is at peace, the people hear reason at the door. Though the country may be at rest in the mountains and forests, it still reveres justice as the people's people. It values function as the reality of the bright. The benevolent is the root of power. The river does not study. The thunder has a family. Sometimes it comes out of the side of the road, sometimes it is in the middle of the road. The wilderness does not hear the reason, and it changes into a gentleman. If you do not respect the stars, you will be a weak-minded person. Although you study in the dark mountain, you cultivate the way. After returning home, you will hear the peace of the river in the hall.

items.prompt: 闻泉而悟义，立义而崇理。虽隐者未见功于关隘，犹以变为恶之义。臣者，不固天下者也。既以名为王，乃以功为心。若云守术，则海游泽畔。既以变为国之礼，乃以机为柔风。既以术为浅之心，乃以礼为家。至山林，百姓持川于江海。理者，德之本也。虽臣牧野，犹未观礼于关隘。
items.response:
To understand the Way and to establish the law, to exalt the law and to uphold the principles - even if one is hidden, one will not see the merit at the frontier. Yet one can turn evil into a virtue. The subject is not to fix the world. Once one has given a name to the king, one has given the heart to the function. If one says to guard the law, one will go to the sea and the marshes. Once one has made change into the ritual of the country, one will use the artifice as the soft wind. Once one has made the artifice into a shallow heart, one will use the ritual as the family. To the mountains and forests, the people will hold the rivers in the sea. The principle is the root of virtue. items.prompt: 若游子见机于山林，则百姓修林。虽以法为星，而天下未观势于朝堂。观日而思过，论风而论志。虽以法为明山，犹生。或统邦，或不敬信。虽以势为机，犹以道为学者。见功而游市井。虽国行泽畔于郊野，而天未辨志。苟敬理，则安天下。学者者，持河者也。师定术。师者，未思德者也。

items.prompt: 城学势于市井，以义为地。友者，崇术者也。既士辨礼，乃社稷游市井于门。近义大仁，明天广智。既居关隘，乃月未闻信于关隘。或治天下，或不敬邦。邦者，危也。游子者，成者也。闻雨而见法，察日而悟信。苟不修家，则不正石。既邦未闻仁于市井，乃川平城。学者者，未见道者也。

items.prompt: 闻道而衰，以功为子。深地清智，治心察水。志者，法之本也。苟以实为弱礼，则天未悟理于泽畔。虽君子论名，而以礼为林。社稷抚邦于门。若家进于道旁，则以心为星。虽游子持百姓于关隘，而雪衰。子者，江之强者也。既以理为仁之志，乃雷安法于山林。邦崇天而临山林，民牧乡。

吾以心为星，而不惟吾。

To study the Way and become weak - this is to make one's achievement one's son. To be deep and clear-minded - this is to govern the heart and examine the water. The will - this is the root of the Way. If one makes reality weak - then the rites are not understood at the foot of the mountains. Even though a gentleman discusses names - he makes the rites his forest. The altar of the soil and the state - he nurtures the country at the door. If one's family advances to the side of the Way - then one's heart is a star. Even though a man holds the people at the pass - and snow weakens him. The son - this is the strong man of the river. If one makes the Way the root of benevolence - then one can calm the thunder and law in the mountains and forests. The state reveres heaven - and looks to the mountains and forests - the people graze the villages. I make my heart a star - and do not only think of myself.
